name: Panbroiled Steak with Whiskey Sauce
recipeIngredient: Large steak, 2 inches thick (or 4 small steaks, 1 inch thick)
Coarse rock-salt or table salt (1/2 teaspoon)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour off any fat from cooking the steak.
2. Melt butter in the same skillet over low heat; add onion and garlic and cook slowly until soft.
3. Add remaining ingredients for the whiskey sauce and simmer for a minute or two.
4. Pour the sauce over the steaks.
5. Trim excess fat from outside the steak.
6. Press crushed peppercorns and garlic into both sides of the meat and let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
7. Heat a cast-iron skillet over high heat. Grease the bottom of the hot skillet with a piece of fat trimmed off the steak. Add about half of the salt.
8. Toss in the steak or steaks quickly on one side, then lift out, add the remaining salt to the skillet, and turn the steak.
9. Reduce the heat to medium and cook the steaks as desired. Remove to a warm platter.
10. Pour sauce over steaks and serve.
